description: Runs a database migration to create or drop the inlay_source_log table and its composite index, using SQLAlchemy.
Generate a single PDF from all coaching playbooks using ReportLab.
Strips brand identity from .pptx/.ppsx files by removing images, blanking text and metadata, and deleting custom shapes, then outputs clean .potx template files.
Check a .potx or .pptx file for leftover brand names, forbidden shapes, unwanted parts, and parse errors after sanitization.
Build a single versatile slide master by slicing 20 chosen layouts out of a sanitized PowerPoint template, renumbering them, and dropping all slides and redundant layouts.
Scans all .pptx files in a given directory and reports a table of layout inventory per file, plus a canonical-coverage score showing how well each file's layouts cover 14 essential layout purposes.
Query and manage the ACME certificate reconciliation process, including viewing status, elements, flows, diffs, fixes, and exporting diagrams to PDF, SVG, BPMN, VSDX, DrawIO, or Mermaid formats.
FastAPI application that mounts routers for health, engagements, natural language intake, personas, and artifacts endpoints, and runs database migrations on startup.
Inserts longform source URLs from Lilian Weng's blog, MIT OCW 6.034, and NIST AI RMF into a SQLite database for deep content ingestion.
Seeds Wikipedia article URLs as depth_hint=2 sources for L2 verbatim content, and lowers WEF report URLs from depth_hint=3 to 2.

Repairs malformed JSON from LLM output by stripping orphan string entries, fixing trailing commas, inserting missing brackets, and escaping unescaped control characters, then returns the repaired JSON and any orphaned text.
Detect content freshness by extracting changelog entries from new or stale source chunks and queueing topic updates based on cosine similarity.

Seeds German-locale Microsoft Learn module URLs for the copilot-m365 topic and support.microsoft.com per-app Copilot URLs for six per-app topics (Word, Excel, PowerPoint, Outlook, Teams, OneNote) into a SQLite database, inserting source_url and routing_rule rows idempotently.
Seeds German-language and university sources (Wikipedia, AI portals, and international universities) for verbatim data collection at L3 DE and L4 EN/DE levels.
Generate role-differentiated content for syllabus topics, targeting roles with missing content types (akademisch, standard, or einfach), with options for a specific role or all roles.
Generate weekly Provider Intelligence Briefs as PDF files with JSON sidecar files for specified providers.
Generates and stores embeddings for all active topics using nomic-embed-text via Ollama, optionally re-embedding even if embeddings already exist.
Seeds depth_hint=4 sources for 17 German and 12 English L4 verbatim gaps by bumping vendor doc depths and inserting new MS Learn URLs.
Inserts topic-role relationships for six Copilot app sub-topics using scores from Microsoft documentation, running idempotently after seed_copilot_apps.py.

Generates long-form documents (L2–L5) for syllabus topics, using verbatim source chunks when available at the requested depth, otherwise falling back to LLM generation from the previous depth's document.
Generate a weekly freshness PDF report from provider_changelog data, using a SQLite database and a Jinja2 template rendered to PDF via WeasyPrint.

Adds seed source URLs and routing rules to fill content gaps for 36 topics that currently have zero attributed content, then instructs the user to run subsequent crawl and document generation scripts.
Seeds the copilot-m365 topic and its job-title relevance scores into the database, safe to run multiple times.
Resets content generation queue items that have been 'in progress' for longer than a configurable threshold back to 'pending' status, and reports how many items were reset.
Assigns relevance scores to topics based on job roles, with optional debug logging.
Generate a self-contained HTML maturity matrix from a JSON descriptor, optionally as a timeline or scrollytelling story.
Seeds MS Learn unit-level URLs into a database for modules whose overview pages yield no content, so that actual lesson text can be scraped.
Sets depth hints on source URLs: marks provider IDs 8, 17, 21 as depth 3, and inserts 22 Microsoft Learn module URLs with depth 4 into a SQLite database.
Seeds sub-topics for Copilot 365 apps under the copilot-m365 parent topic in a SQLite database, using INSERT OR IGNORE for idempotency.
Seeds a database from an XLSX syllabus file by extracting noun chunks and populating topic, source, and keyword tables, skipping if the file hash hasn't changed.

Launch a Streamlit UI for the MeetingProcessor tool, which loads configuration, selects a PPTX export mode, builds format and delivery exporters, and provides tabs for processing meetings, viewing usage stats, managing settings, and running a demo.
Syncs metadata from Poster Engine artifact YAML files into a database table, using the persona and artifact ID as a unique slug, without copying body text.
Seeds the publication table from a YAML file, inserting only new rows by slug and preserving existing operator-edited columns.
Ingest the O*NET text database into onet_* backbone tables (occupation, task, dwa, and task↔dwa), using real O*NET 30.3 column headers and INSERT OR IGNORE for idempotency.
Reads topics from a v1 database (read-only) and imports them into a v2 database, matching topics to existing groups by slug or placing them in a 'v1-legacy-holding' group.

Reads a SQLite database path from the command line and inserts 6 hardcoded Architecture Decision Records (ADR-001 through ADR-006) into the decision_scope_kind and decision tables, skipping rows that already exist.
Ingest the ESCO↔O*NET crosswalk CSV into a database table, handling a preamble, quoted multi-line fields, categorical match confidence, and deduplication.

Ingest the Anthropic Economic Index task prior into the onet_task_penetration table by normalizing task names (lowercase, strip trailing period, collapse whitespace) and left-joining to O*NET task text, logging coverage and handling collisions deterministically.

Seeds the 'ai-automation-default' maturity model into a SQLite database by inserting one model row, 17 axes (8 TOM + 9 NFR), and tier shells for each axis, using hardcoded data and ensuring idempotency on model slug and axis slug.
Seeds the topic_role_rel table with mock relevance scores for job titles only, using per-topic JSON fixtures, and is idempotent via INSERT OR IGNORE.

Seeds a v2 SQLite database from a CORE SYLLABUS XLSX file, populating curriculum tables like topic_group, topic, source, topic_source, source_lesson, and topic_keyword, while skipping sheets without matching sources and using idempotent INSERT OR IGNORE logic.

Reads pending manifest files from a cache directory, applies their diff and events to a local database, then moves each manifest to an ingested or failed directory. Returns exit codes 0–4 to indicate success or specific failure modes.
Captures a live HTML fixture from the heise archive for a given slug, year, and month, saving it to the test fixtures directory with a header containing capture metadata.
Performs a one-time login to Heise SSO using credentials from a local markdown file and saves the session cookie jar to a specific path.
Regenerates a markdown file that maps acceptance criteria (A1–A15) to the pytest tests covering them, by scanning all test files for @pytest.mark.ac(...) decorators.

Checks that every acceptance criterion (AC) in the heise-archive spec has at least one test decorated with @pytest.mark.ac, printing uncovered ACs to stderr and exiting with code 1 if any are missing.
Backfill missing authority scores for vendor artefacts by using an LLM (DeepSeek) to judge them, with options for dry-run, limiting rows, and overriding a monthly token budget.
Crawls six specific German support.microsoft.com Copilot pages and runs them through the existing ingest pipeline (fetch, chunk, embed, route), skipping unrelated RSS feeds.

Upgrades the provenance of German-language Copilot topics for six Office apps by changing their content source type from 'llm_generated' to 'llm_derived' if attribution data exists, otherwise logs a warning.
Run the nightly syllabus ingest DAG, optionally specifying a SQLite database path and enabling debug logging.
Restores the syllabus.db database from a specified snapshot file, optionally pruning old snapshots to keep only the most recent ones.
Batch generate role content for all topics that have uncovered topic-role pairs, running generate_role_content.py per topic for each register in dependency order.
Moves one or more Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) from 'proposed' to 'accepted' status by updating their YAML frontmatter, optionally writing changes to disk instead of just previewing them.
Creates timestamped snapshots of a SQLite database file and keeps only the most recent two, deleting older ones.
Reads a role set's slice competence IDs from its canonical ground-truth YAML file, not from the database.
Generate an index.html file from a README.md, incorporating recent additions and updates.
Extract URLs from text using regex pattern and return them as a list.
Generate documentation for HTML files in a repository using Claude, with options to specify a repository path, enable verbose output, or perform a dry run.
Generate an HTML page that lists all tools grouped by their creation month.
Reads a _redirects.json file and generates HTML redirect pages for each source-to-target mapping.
Formats a commit message by escaping HTML, converting URLs into clickable links, and linking issue references like #123.
Generate a JSON file mapping HTML files to their most recent commit dates.
Reads a config.yaml file, extracts all tool collections, and writes dist/tools.json, dist/index.html, and dist/llms.txt; supports a --dry-run flag to count without writing.
Tag tools from a JSON file by matching their descriptions against patterns in a YAML vocabulary file, outputting tagged results and unmatched entries.
Assigns each tool to a hex cell in a honeycomb mosaic, reading tagged tools and optional anchors, then writing a layout JSON.
Read meeting data from a file, Gmail message, or search query and generate Office documents (docx, xlsx, pptx) in a specified output directory.

Ingests provider capability data from a source file into a database, using a deterministic pass-1 process that writes audit rows in separate transactions to preserve failure evidence, and translates numeric outcomes to outcome codes.
Derive provider_capability rows from a source table by matching provider names to existing providers, then inserting capability records with a unique code, title, and source path.
Creates the competence vocabulary schema and seeds initial levels in a SQLite database, using a cross-platform file lock to ensure idempotent execution.
FastAPI backend for MeetingProcessor, shared by Classical and Solar frontends, that validates endpoint URLs against a provider allowlist and lazily evicts expired CSRF states.
Regenerate a MANIFEST.sha256 file from all system_*.md files in a prompts directory.
Generate ToolHub translation tables by translating Chinese tool descriptions to English via DeepSeek, optionally resuming from existing output.
Find recent .jsonl files and optionally publish one as a Gist.
Debug S3 access issues for a given URL by checking bucket existence, region, key existence, object metadata, versions, ACLs, bucket policy, and encryption settings.
Extract files of specified MIME types from a HAR archive.
Extracts and decodes a base64-encoded source map from a JavaScript file, outputting the decoded content to stdout.
Checks a given URL for Gzip, ETag, and Last-Modified support using only Python's standard library, printing results to stdout and exiting with code 1 on critical errors.
Inspects Terminal.app tabs and reports their RAM usage, with options to limit displayed tabs, command groups, and processes, include itself in totals, or output JSON.
Process a PDF file using Mistral's OCR API and output the results as Markdown, HTML, or JSON, with optional image handling.
Generate audio from text using Qwen3-TTS and MLX Audio, saving to a unique file path.
Streams a markdown response from an LLM model to a Textual terminal app, optionally showing usage info.
Extracts all valid JSON objects from text (file or stdin) using a state machine parser and outputs them as Python dictionaries.
Read a GGUF file and dump all metadata key/value pairs as YAML (default) or JSON (with --json). Accepts a file path and optional --exclude prefix.
Lists available model IDs from OpenAI, Anthropic, and Gemini by fetching API keys via 'llm keys get' and querying each provider's API, skipping any provider whose key retrieval fails.
Displays an image inline in the terminal, automatically resizing it to fit the terminal width and height without upscaling.
Fetch file listing of every available Google Cloud bucket and report total sizes.
Start a background HTTP server serving a given directory on a given port, or stop the server using a stored PID file.
Replace whitespace-only lines with blank lines in text files, optionally previewing changes without modifying files.
Serves a git repository over HTTP as a read-only server using git-http-backend, taking a path to the repository and an optional port number.
Fetches network status from an ASUS ZenWiFi XT8 router via its HTTP API, using the same appGet.cgi endpoint as the mobile app, and outputs a human-readable summary or raw JSON.
Converts Claude Code JSONL conversation logs into readable Markdown files, formatting timestamps, tool use, tool results, and message content.
Detects invisible text in a PDF, such as transparent, off-page, zero-size, or white text, by analyzing the document with PyMuPDF.
Reads text from stdin and a list of search terms as arguments, then outputs colored matches with surrounding context.
Extracts unique issue numbers (like #1234) from git commit messages within a specified tag range, returning them as a comma-separated list.
Creates a timelapse GIF (or silent MP4) from a YouTube livestream's DVR by capturing frames at intervals over a specified time range.
Manage S3 buckets with DynamoDB metadata tracking, including creating buckets with Lambda triggers and listing files via DynamoDB.
Calculates the total size of a ModelScope model by fetching its file list from the API and converting the result to gigabytes.
Lists visible macOS windows with filtering by minimum size, owner name, and HUD/overlay status using CoreGraphics.
Generate an image using OpenAI's image models, with a required prompt and optional output file path, printing metadata to stderr and saving the image.
Convert Codex JSONL session logs to Markdown format from a specified .jsonl file.

Inspect a Wasmer WebC archive (.webc) and print summary information including a human-readable byte count. Accepts a path to the archive and an optional integer for the number of initial bytes to scan when locating the root CBOR map.
Run an OpenAI Responses API background prompt, poll for completion, and print the final response JSON.
Combines multiple JSON command definition files from a Redis source directory into a single sorted JSON file, adding metadata about file origins.
Starts a cross-device multiplayer Pong game server that serves a web-based game and supports WebSocket connections for real-time multiplayer play.
Starts the ComfyUI server, returning the event loop, server instance, and an async start function.

A command-line tool for launching ComfyUI with extensive configuration options for networking, GPU/CPU settings, memory management, model precision, custom node loading, and frontend version selection.
Manage frontend package versions: validate, check, download, and initialize frontend assets.
Generates .pyi stub files for synchronous API wrappers, allowing stub creation without running the full ComfyUI application.
Syncs GLSL shader files between the current folder and blueprint JSON files, either extracting shaders from JSONs or patching them back in.
Connects to ComfyUI's API to expose workflow tools (view, edit, run workflows; manage nodes, custom nodes, and models) to Claude Code via MCP.
Creates and returns the path to an API log directory inside ComfyUI's temp folder, ensuring it exists.
Runs the forward pass of the HunYuan ControlNet encoder, processing image, timestep, and text embeddings to produce control outputs.
Takes image tensors, text embeddings, and metadata tensors, runs the encoder forward pass, and returns output tensors (e.g., patch embeddings).
Runs the consigliere CLI application, which accepts command-line arguments and produces text output.
Scrapes Google Maps for local business data based on a search query and saves the results to a CSV file.
Seeds a SQLite database with two predefined UX capability entries for the Idea Forge, using an idempotent script that can be run repeatedly without duplicating data.
Export entity profiles from a SQLite database to markdown files in a specified output directory, with an optional facts database.

Builds a large test fixture for the graph5 quarantine feature by generating 160 Mastodon API JSON files (directory pages, statuses, and hashtag statuses) from a declarative seed map, used to test seed qualification under a 2-of-3 gate.
Generates a minimal test fixture (5 instances, 1 empty API response each) for the §13.3 quarantine determinism test, to be committed alongside spec changes.

Checks that the quality-baseline.json file for the NGO export pipeline meets a precision threshold and has drift within 10%, exiting with a specific code for success, failure, or missing/malformed file.
Register Mastodon OAuth apps on 5 curated instances, writing client credentials and bearer tokens to an env file, with a dry-run mode by default.
Seeds a SQLite database with 6 predefined NGO network entities and their relationships, idempotently.
Generates a pytest smoke-test stub for a given Alembic migration file, or runs a self-test to verify stub generation works.

Reference event loop that reads handoff requests from stdin and routes them to managed agents, outputting steering decisions as text. Input is a JSON string with handoff payload; output is text describing the routing result.
Validates a JSON output file against a JSON or YAML schema, exiting with code 0 if valid or 1 if invalid (with an error message to stderr).
A command-line tool that manages a pipeline for acquiring lecture transcripts, extracting schemas, compiling them into skill markdown files, and deploying them to Claude's commands directory.
Analyze a reference video for production replication, with options to force re-analysis and specify an output directory.
Show FLM pipeline status by displaying total leads and counts grouped by status.
Manage a business process catalog: fetch ESCO occupations, seed challenges, generate and review automation scenarios, run automatch, and discover business process challenges.
Collects and aggregates market signals from multiple channels, manages taxonomy data, and generates research reports.
Manage investment portfolios: create, list, import companies, run OSINT investigations, generate synthesis reports, and validate log files.
A command-line tool for submitting, polling, consuming, and re-consuming batches in a portfolio sync workflow, with subcommands for each stage.
Manage entity memory layers, compress stale summaries, and display staleness matrices.

Manage network analysis graphs: list, create, show metrics, and build specific graph versions (graph-4 and graph-5) from curated Mastodon instances, with scaffolding that validates flags but defers live extraction.
CLI subcommand group for the Strategic Brain proactive scheduler: view next actions, toggle kill-switch, check status, trigger a pipeline tick, or generate a daily autonomy-budget summary.
Red Flag Analyzer CLI commands for managing entity risk analysis, including running analyses, viewing history, and batch processing workbook files.
Configure CLI runtime settings for token-efficient output, including global flags, logging levels, a shared Rich console, and patching of echo functions to enforce output limits.
List entity profiles from the local OSINT database. Outputs a text list of profile names.
Search the web using Vertex AI Search, returning structured results (URL, title, snippet) for ad-hoc research, with configurable scope and result count.

Manage speaker network data: pull social follows, subscribe to GitHub repos, reconcile database references, revoke handles, backfill missing handles via search, check pipeline health, and reverify existing handles against live profiles.
Consigliere CLI manages ingestion, status, plugins, database operations, and various discovery/enrichment pipelines for a unified ingest engine.
Manage topic research: run searches, fetch articles, create dossiers, and poll or list results via CLI commands.
Promotes quarantine data into normalized tables and extracts signals, typically invoked by Dagu DAGs (e.g., `pipeline run --source-type etsy_monitor`).
Parses a natural language research question, asks clarifying questions if needed, and runs OSINT investigations.
Runs three database checks to verify it is safe for INGEST-02 Phase 3 migration, exiting with 0 if all pass or with a non-zero status, row-count summary, and remediation hint on failure.
Manage strategic brain suggestions, patterns, digests, and briefs via CLI commands that accept arguments like suggestion IDs, domain names, or no arguments for listing.
Manage a UX design corpus: sync from a curated list, discover and ingest guidelines from Vertex, list, show, or search design documents.
Manage ideas in the Idea Forge incubator: add, list, view, link, merge, graduate, enrich, cluster, deduplicate, and generate specs.
Fetches posts from a source (e.g., Reddit) filtered by a topic slug, extracts voice insights, and outputs them as a table or JSON.
Discovers and provides built-in and entry-point-based modules for the Consigliere application, controlled by the CONSIGLIERE_MODULES_MODE environment variable.
Registers core API routers (health, items, plugins) into a FastAPI application using the module registry system.

Defines a Python Protocol for modules that register CLI commands (via typer) and API routers (via FastAPI) into a shared AppContext. This interface is used to compose CLI and API functionality from multiple modules.
Manage process discovery workflows, including listing, viewing, exporting, and ingesting process models and signals.

Deduplicates entity profiles by merging duplicate rows based on (entity_type, lower(canonical_name)) collisions, normalizes non-conforming entity_type values, and writes a JSON report. Inputs: optional database path and report directory; outputs: a JSON report file.
Extracts qualified seeds from a quarantine directory by applying a followers threshold and rel=me verification, then writes the results to qualified_seeds.json.
Build graph-5 database rows (network_graph, entity_profile, graph_node, PlatformIdentity) from a quarantine directory's qualified_seeds.json, with optional dry-run and fixture update.
Extracts the Google I/O 2026 speaker roster from io.google into a quarantine snapshot, using a browser to navigate, wait for shadow DOM content, and save structured data with an audit trail.
Compares two extraction.json files and exits with an error if their Jaccard similarity is below 0.96, used by operators to verify reproducibility between two fresh extractions.
Builds database rows for a network graph from a quarantine extraction, filtering speakers and creating platform identity records.
Validates a user's Entra ID token and returns the skills and MCP servers that employee is allowed to use.
Mint a self-signed dev token for local testing, generating or reusing RSA keys and JWKS files.
Prints your Entra (Azure AD) tenant ID by looking up a domain or email, or by using the Azure CLI if logged in.
Generate branded scaffold download files for artefacts based on their system prompts.
Generates a 1200x630 Open Graph card image (og-default.png) with a dark background, DM Serif Display wordmark, and accent stripe, used for both og:image and JSON-LD Organization.logo.
Generate per-artifact system prompts in German and English from structured artifact data, without using an LLM.
Regenerate pre-built binary masters (reference.docx/pptx) from global.css, or check for drift with --check flag.
Resolves input files into a run manifest, then renders each file to the specified output format using Chrome.

Opens a copy of an existing office file (DOCX, XLSX, PPTX) and injects brand chrome (title, about, process, annex, glossary sections and Red-Black-Cinema theme) while preserving the authored body content; also canonicalizes XLSX styles to avoid Excel repair dialogs. Input is a manifest JSON and source directory; output is a branded file written to the output directory.

Validates that Office Open XML files (.docx/.xlsx/.pptx) would open cleanly in MS Office by strictly parsing all XML parts with lxml, rejecting empty comments stubs, and reloading through the format library to catch structural breakage.
Routes semantic content and format through the matching brand master to produce an output file.

Generates a Word reference document (reference.docx) by applying Office design system styles from office_tokens.py to pandoc's default reference.docx, ensuring all required styles (Table, Compact, FirstParagraph, BodyText, etc.) are present for valid Word output.

Audits the type scale (title/body point sizes and ratio ladder) of a PowerPoint file or a directory of PowerPoint files, optionally validating against an expected ratio ladder, and outputs results as a table or JSON.
Creates a themed PowerPoint reference file for pandoc by modifying the default reference deck's theme fonts, accent color, and slide-master text styles via XML blob manipulation.
Harvest brand/process terminology from structured TOML files and prose conventions into a generated terminology.md, with a --check flag to detect drift from sources.

Regenerates or verifies the docs/pages-to-data.json file by walking pages, extracting timestamps, and optionally enriching data via a QMD lookup; exits with specific codes for stale data, schema errors, unsupported grammar, or unreachable QMD.
Authenticate to the All-Inkl KAS SOAP API using customer credentials and return a 40-character session token, or raise an error on failure.
Generates a roadmap HTML page from a BACKLOG.md file and archived 'done' items for the getaccess.eu project.
Migrates static HTML pages from a local 'deploy/site/projects' directory to WordPress via its REST API, extracting main content and creating pages with URL slugs.
Converts HTML articles from getaccess.net to markdown files with frontmatter, skipping those already converted.
Applies design system CSS variable replacements (font, border-radius, box-shadow, nav background) to a single article HTML file, removing inline font declarations and old Google Fonts links.
Injects a theme system (CSS, JS, toggle button) into getaccess.net article HTML files, removes theme-owned CSS variables from inline :root blocks, and skips files that have already been processed.

Migrate legacy gtxs.eu HTML articles into Astro markdown collections by extracting Type A articles (with <article class="content">) into .md files with YAML frontmatter, and reporting Type B files to be copied separately.
Generate a roadmap HTML page from a backlog Markdown file and archived done items for the getaccess.eu project.
Migrate static HTML pages from a local directory to WordPress pages via REST API, extracting main content and creating pages with a specified hierarchy.

Converts a single HTML article from getaccess.net to markdown with frontmatter, writing it to the articles-md/ directory. Returns True if the article was written, False if it was skipped because a .md file already exists.

Reads legacy HTML articles from a source directory, extracts Type A articles (with <article class="content">) into .md files with YAML frontmatter in a destination directory, and returns a status report for each file.
Deletes all but the newest N snapshot directories inside a given folder, returning the names of the deleted directories.
Walks an Astro dist/ directory and outputs one public URL per HTML file, optionally writing to a file instead of stdout.
A long-lived worker that reads line-delimited JSON requests from stdin, processes them using registered engines, and writes JSON responses to stdout, exiting on parse failure.
Run read-only SQL queries against a telemetry SQLite database, outputting results as JSON or text.
Reads a SQLite database (keto_score.db) and writes a JSONL golden-set file for the keto-categorize eval consumer, selecting the first --per-cat foods per category ordered by name_en.

Reads a consumer's golden set of test cases from a JSONL file, runs them through a candidate model on a live router, scores responses using deterministic matching (exact/regex/contains), and returns a JSON result with pass/fail status and gating decision based on a baseline threshold.
Scans Python code for direct provider API access (e.g., Anthropic, OpenAI) that should be routed through a local router, flagging violations only for providers listed in a guard/armed.yaml file.

Builds one markdown file per use-case theme by parsing KNOWN_PATTERNS.md entries by their Category field and combining them with portable rules from CLAUDE.md, DOR.md, DOD.md, and memory feedback files.
Reads a file of corrected user story body text and replaces the corresponding As a / I want / so that lines in BACKLOG.md for each matched story ID.
Reads Codex DOR upgrade output files and applies user story sentences and state promotions to BACKLOG.md.

A git pre-commit hook that blocks commits introducing new CLI scripts without required paired memory files, with fail-open on infrastructure errors, an escape valve via environment variable, and an arm self-check precondition.
Generates a markdown report of automation debt by bucketing pattern-key occurrences into weekly windows, using the same grammar as the producer to parse BACKLOG.md #Done markers and completion dates.
Runs a dry-run orchestration of stages A through F for eligible auto-debt tickets, posting a shadow decision comment without merging, and performing reconciliation at startup.
Accepts a ticket identifier and an approve/reject verdict, appends a ledger entry to the shadow ledger file for that ticket, and outputs a confirmation message.
Adds a new user story to the backlog, generating the next US-ID under a given prefix and inserting the story template with acceptance criteria.
Reads BACKLOG.md, moves completed (Done) story blocks into BACKLOG-ARCHIVE.md grouped by project, and writes both files back. Default is dry-run (no writes); pass --apply to perform the move.
Scans git logs for feat() commits referencing user story IDs, cross-checks against BACKLOG.md to find implemented stories not marked as Done, and outputs a reconciliation report.
Checks that the staged BACKLOG.md file's State section matches the event log, rejecting hand edits that diverge from logged events.
Reads a BACKLOG.md file and Paperclip tickets, then creates or updates Paperclip tickets to match the backlog items, returning matched tickets, unmatched backlog items, and manual tickets.
Appends a single insight (with optional context and key-point flag) to a JSONL file at ~/.local/state/insights/pending.jsonl for later review.
Marks a story in a BACKLOG.md file as 'In-Progress' using FileGuard, given the backlog path, story ID, and session ID.
Reads MEMORY.md, truncates each entry's hook text to a maximum character limit, and writes the modified content back to MEMORY.md using atomic file replacement.
Checks whether a CLI command exists, returning exit code 0 if it does, 1 if it does not, or 2 on error.
Checks whether an inline script pattern has been removed from a codebase, returning exit code 0 if the pattern is absent, 1 if still present, or 2 on error.
Verifies that a previously unused skill is now referenced in the codebase, exiting 0 if the gap is closed, 1 if still present, or 2 on error.
Analyze DeepSeek API usage logs to report reasoning ratios and cache hit rates per caller, flagging potential issues like disabled thinking or broken prefix caching.

Reads HANDOVER files from the past 7 days, deduplicates KNOWN_PATTERNS.md, and commits changes tagged with a run ID. Triggered by /lightsout or daily cron, accepts optional flags for mode, dry-run, max age, or processing all files.
Enrich autogenerated about.md files by replacing placeholder text with high-confidence facts from local wiki exports, QMD context, and port manager metadata.

Manages an append-only event store for story status transitions, supporting operations like adding events, retrieving the latest or all events for a story, deriving the current stage, and checking consistency against expected snapshots.
Generate a markdown table showing which capabilities each agent has, with color-coded indicators, and optionally publish it to a wiki.
Generates a start/stop/continue retro report skeleton from governance baselines, optionally specifying the governance directory.
Runs all P1-P7 governance patterns and the KP-to-baseline linker, typically scheduled as a daily scan at 06:00 via Dagu.

Scans a draft handover or TODO file for US-ID tokens, checks their git status across known repos, flags stale TODO files older than 24 hours, and surfaces recently modified HANDOVER files for cross-check, always exiting with code 0.
Runs 100 consecutive write cycles using FileGuard and reports P50/P95/P99 latency values in milliseconds.
Fetch a URL and return its content as markdown, with SSRF protection, optional readability extraction, and caching.
Scans a local projects directory for non-index.html HTML entrypoints (like Vite/React dev files), zips each with its index.html, and serves an inventory JSON over HTTP for iPad access.
Emits a JSON report of verification checks with PASS, FAIL, or SKIP statuses.
Generate a Homer dashboard config.yml from a portmgr services API, optionally printing to stdout (dry-run) or comparing against a backup file.
Checks that escalation channels (inbox_md, handover_warning, osascript, paperclip_issue) are reachable, emits a BLOCKED situation report, and notifies all channels of the blockage.
Manage incident lifecycle: create new incidents, list active ones, transfer incident command, and close incidents.
Migrates an incident's archived or runtime contract to a new ICS schema version, preserving the original as a backup.
Abort an incident by writing a cancellation status and SITREP stub, or escalate it by sending notifications across configured channels.

Creates Paperclip issues with read-back enforcement and dispatches work orders after checking runtime contract status, proxying to paperclip.sh. Inputs are incident ID, title, optional body file, and dispatch parameters; outputs are command exit codes and text output.

Reads the runtime contract for an incident and emits a §5 read-back block (Objective, Exit criteria, Out of scope, Constraints, Deltas, Confirmation) as markdown or JSON, optionally persisting it to a sitreps directory.
Tests whether a language model surfaces seeded contradictions in ICS-202 contracts during a read-back protocol, scoring responses for falsifiability.

Generates an ICS-209-equivalent situation report (SITREP) for an incident, with status inferred from the contract state or overridden by a flag, and outputs it as markdown, JSON, or persisted to disk.
Resolves disagreements between reviewers by opening a unified command sub-contract, arbitrating each disputed comment, and logging the rulings.

Generates a deterministic inventory of infrastructure categories (skills, Dagu DAGs with schedules, and allocated ports) for the Automation Engineer's Knowledge Base, intended for scheduled Dagu job execution.
Enable, disable, or check the status of the Stop-hook janitor that cleans dirty worktrees on Claude Code Stop events.
Installs or uninstalls a scheduled task (launchd, cron, or Task Scheduler) to run the worktree-janitor every 2 hours on the current platform.
Checks if the janitor log file has been updated within the last 3 hours, and files a P2 backlog alert if it hasn't, to catch silent failures of the janitor's scheduled runs.
Assigns KP numbers to placeholder KP-TBD blocks in a markdown file and appends them to KNOWN_PATTERNS.md, or prints the blocks that would be appended without writing.

A one-time script that triages and migrates existing automation debt from a pending.jsonl file by generating a dry-run report (Phase A), culling duplicates and stale entries (Phase B), or handing off remaining items to Rudi (Phase C).
One-time script to drain existing Paperclip ticket back-pressure by triaging, bulk-closing duplicates/stale tickets, and demoting remaining high/critical tickets to medium.
Migrates old pending.jsonl entries to a new schema by splitting clean heartbeats into a separate file, normalizing remaining entries, and validating them against the v1 schema.
Generates today's Morning Brief into a specified state directory (default: ~/.local/state/morning-brief/) and prints the path to the written file.
Fetches all n8n node metadata from the API, enriches each node with its top template description and view count, and caches the result for 23 hours.
Reads an n8n capabilities JSON file and generates a self-contained HTML file that renders an interactive, infinite-canvas map of capability clusters, which can be opened directly in a browser.
Tags all 566 n8n nodes with capabilities from a taxonomy by sending a single batch request to a Hermes LLM API, discarding any previous tags.
Syncs completed Paperclip issues (status=done) with a BACKLOG.md file by marking matching user story blocks as shipped, updating their state line and ticking checkboxes.
Detects handover files written by other Claude sessions running concurrently, outputting JSON with sibling file details and using exit codes to indicate whether parallel handovers exist.
Merges multiple parallel-session HANDOVER markdown files into one merged file, using the largest file as the primary and appending deduplicated sections from the others.
Parse a BACKLOG.md file into structured JSON for the Governance Dashboard, with configurable input and output paths.
Reconciles registered pipeline entries against disk and detects schema drift between two registries, reporting discrepancies without making any changes.
Scans a directory tree for Git repositories and reports their status, optionally filtering by depth, excluded paths, and uncommitted changes.
Reads enabled allocations from portmgr.db, resolves each to a local app root, checks for a valid about.md file, and optionally creates a Paperclip issue with the findings.

Seeds a portmgr SQLite database with groups and Homer entry metadata from a config.yml snapshot, and repairs legacy NOT NULL constraints on port/assigned_at columns to allow alias/page/external inserts.
Serves a local projects directory over HTTP, registering itself with a port manager service.

Reads a YAML registry of pattern-keys with weekly recurrence data, and for each key that has recurred in at least two consecutive weekly windows after a fix marker, generates a verifier stub script, registers it in a YAML registry, and files a backlog story via backlog_add.py.
Promote pending insights from pending.jsonl to KNOWN_PATTERNS.md by filtering, deduplicating, classifying, appending new patterns, archiving entries, and truncating the pending file.
Filter, classify, and secret-scan publishable items from a markdown file, outputting a JSON manifest of items that pass allow/block lists and secret scanning.
Starts an HTTP server that wraps the qmd CLI, providing a status endpoint and a query endpoint to search a collection.
Runs a multi-stage quality gate on in-review user-story tickets, checking DoD compliance, code review, and architecture panels, then updates the ticket status and labels based on the results.
Scans QMD project collections for improvement opportunities, runs analysis lenses via Ollama, and generates a daily report with KAIZEN.md and INBOX triage.
Re-stamps age badges (like [5d]) on all US-* headings in a backlog file, computing each age as the number of days since the first git commit that introduced that US ID.
Scans a directory for about.md files and repairs broken frontmatter lines that look like '\1"YYYY-MM-DD"' by replacing them with 'about_last_reviewed: "YYYY-MM-DD"'.

Reads unassigned automation-debt tickets from a todo list, groups them by content hash, and creates BACKLOG user stories via backlog_add.py, with optional dry-run mode that skips story creation and ticket status transitions.
Classifies a Claude Code session's tool calls into categories (ceremony, meta, discovery, impl, verify, glue, other) and reports the shape ratio; read-only.

Runs live integration tests against the Paperclip API to verify that the backlog_sync_paperclip tool's list, create, patch, and cleanup operations work correctly, exiting with 0 if all pass or non-zero on first failure.
Find and list unique debt kinds from tickets that are missing a verifier in the registry.
Archive a pending JSONL file into a monthly archive file and clear the pending file.
Sweeps automation-debt pending entries into Paperclip issues and archives them, replacing a manual Step 2b checklist.
Exports Claude Code memory and governance documents into Hermes memory format, splitting content into user and memory files with size limits and optional dry-run or verbose modes.

Scans an inbox folder for task files, validates them against a config, assigns IDs, appends new tasks to an IRL markdown file under correct quadrant headings, and moves completed tasks to a DONE file.

Runs two Tier-1 security checks: verifies that all QMD collection paths exist and are non-empty, and lints all Dagu DAG YAML files for valid syntax, exiting with code 0 if all pass, 1 if any check fails, or 2 on infrastructure errors.
Tests URLs from an inventory JSON file and ToolHub root assets, then prints a summary and writes a health report JSON file.
Validate an app's about.md file against the Innovation Scout contract specification, returning frontmatter and body, with staleness treated as error in CI mode.
Refreshes a portfolio wiki by generating 5 pages per project using an LLM and Wiki.js GraphQL.

Acquires a single-instance lock, discovers worktrees under each configured root, classifies each into one of nine states, resolves an action (destructive or skip), writes a per-run audit markdown file, and exits non-zero if any entry fails.

Compares project state against wiki documentation to detect drift such as new files, API changes, dependency changes, missing projects, or stale pages, and outputs a JSON report and human-readable summary.

A CLI tool that reads and writes Wiki.js pages by directly accessing a local SQLite database. It supports listing pages, getting page content, setting or appending content from files, managing sections, and creating new pages.
Export all Wiki.js pages from SQLite to markdown files with YAML frontmatter, skipping unchanged pages unless forced.
Starts an HTTP server that captures microphone audio, computes a log-frequency binned FFT, and serves the spectrum data via HTTP requests.
Serves a music jukebox web server using Bottle.py, managing a music directory with automatic cleanup of old files when storage is needed.
Runs an OCR server in Termux that accepts image uploads via HTTP POST and returns extracted text, confidence, and fields.
Generates a DATEV EXTF Buchungsstapel (booking batch) CSV file from test receipt data, using format version 700, data category 21, and format version 13.
Build a MAME catalog from a listxml file and optional support files, then save it as JSON.
Summarize rm_ts_server ioctl_shim EVT logs from a given ioctl_trace.log* file.

Takes a depth CSV and an RGB JPEG, runs them through a ladder of vision and text fusion models (Claude Sonnet, Claude Haiku, moondream, Qwen3), and outputs scene understanding descriptions along with cost and performance metrics.
Analyze depth CSV files from the Sony iToF Range Test app, computing statistics and generating an ASCII spatial map of raw millimeter depth values.
Scans directories, archives, and disk images for files related to NVIDIA ShieldTech inputflinger, extracting nested images when deep scan is enabled.
Scans NVIDIA recovery downloads for ShieldTech/inputflinger artifacts, optionally extracting and scanning nested partition images from archives.
Lists GitLab group projects via the public API, filtering by case-insensitive substrings, with pagination safety and configurable per-page limits.

Lists NVIDIA Developer Download Center entries from a JSON file, filtering by title substring (default 'SHIELD'), exact version, 'Developer Only OS Image' in title, and 'dev_rooted' in file URL, then prints entry keys and landing page URLs.
Convert Android OTA transfer list and new.dat files into a raw partition image (e.g., system.img).
Lists files in a stock ROM image that are related to DirectStylus/StylusMobile, including supporting libraries and feature XMLs, using a heuristic based on 7z output.
Reads a LineageOS OTA zip and a partition name, then applies new/erase/zero block commands from the transfer list to produce a raw sparse system image file.
Inspects a system image file to find missing services by listing its contents with 7z and comparing against expected service paths.
Build a MAME catalog from a listxml file and optional support files, then save it as JSON.
Captures raw touch events from a Linux input device and prints parsed tap coordinates and tracking IDs.
A CLI tool to test the Hermes Android app end-to-end, supporting commands for API testing, settings verification, chat messaging, screenshot capture, logcat dumping, and a combined 'all' mode.
A health check endpoint that returns 200 with agent count if the Paperclip cookie is valid, or 503 if the cookie is expired or Paperclip is unreachable, with optional token authentication.
Generates a PDF file from Markdown content or live PostgreSQL database queries, writing the result to disk.
Downloads all food composition data from the BEDCA XML API by querying each food item and saving the results.
Reads a FooDB JSON file and inserts its food and compound data into the enrichment pipeline database in 500-row batches via EnrichmentImportService, with an optional --dry-run flag to skip writes.
Parse UK COFID Group codes and AU AUSNUT Classification codes from raw_data into category_l1, with an optional dry-run mode.
Translate pending food names from Korean, Thai, Vietnamese, Indonesian, Lao, Khmer, and Spanish to English via the DeepSeek API, or approve already-English entries, by updating the database.
Fixes misassigned food categories across all data sources by backing up current values, applying keyword-based reclassification rules, and supporting dry-run, apply, and rollback operations.
Categorizes remaining uncategorized foods (branded and non-branded) using Claude Haiku, with optional dry-run and batch-size settings.
Translate food names from Portuguese or French to English using Claude Haiku, re-extracting names from PDFs and inserting translations into a SQLite database.
Backfills food names in a SQLite database by calling an internal service function.
Maps USDA branded_food_category values from a SQLite database to a unified L1 taxonomy using exact matching and keyword fallback, with an optional dry-run mode.

Export a curated subset of KETO data as a standalone SQLite database for the Flutter app, including top foods by category, scoring nutrients, English names, category metadata, and a data version manifest.
Enriches BLS derivation data in a SQLite database, with an optional dry-run mode to preview changes without committing.

Assigns a second-level food category (category_l2) to foods that already have a first-level category (category_l1) but are missing L2, using a local Ollama LLM with few-shot examples, primarily for Korean and Portuguese food names.
Registers source databases, runs parsers, imports data, and performs spot checks for a specified source or all sources.
Enriches food categories in a SQLite database by running a category enrichment service, with an optional dry-run flag to preview changes without committing.
Assigns L1 food categories to uncategorized foods by matching English food names against a priority-ordered list of keywords, marking each match with a 'keyword_rule' tag for auditability.
Translate Portuguese food names to English and assign L1 taxonomy categories for PT_PORTFIR2025 foods, and report status for BR_TACO4 names.
Orchestrates a 5-phase enrichment pipeline (fetch, import, spot-check, optional link, cleanup) for a single ingest source, collapsing a manual script sequence into one resumable command.
Provides a structured logger for CLI tools that outputs operational telemetry (with timestamps, levels, and structured fields) to stderr, separate from user-facing output.
Imports enrichment data in caller-side chunks (default 500 rows) with preflight validation, reporting aggregated service stats verbatim.
Parse, import, and spot-check food data sources, with options to run for a specific source or all sources, control sample size, skip spot checks, perform dry runs, or continue on error.
Links enrichment foods from a specified source database to canonical foods using exact and fuzzy name matching, replacing all existing links for that source.
Maps source categories to unified L1/L2/L3 hierarchy, optionally showing counts without writing.
Apply a .sql file to the core database in a single transaction.
Merge nutrient target codes from a CSV file into existing enrichment foods, without creating new foods or overwriting existing codes, with optional dry-run and expected-delta validation.
Creates a timestamped snapshot of keto.db, prunes old backups to keep a specified maximum, and optionally labels the backup with a tag.
Run a batch gap-survey across all registered data sources, checking for missing nutrient codes and reporting per-source results plus aggregate totals.
Check database health by reporting row counts, last import time, and connection status.
Parses a playbook markdown file at runtime and prints each step verbatim, also showing the equivalent `keto enrich ingest-source` command for automation-eligible steps (7, 8, 9).
CLI for managing a KETO food composition database with subcommands to populate, quality-check, enrich, and queue data.
Manage a TODO-Today/DONE-Today task lifecycle by moving completed items, archiving done files, and syncing tasks to a backlog.

Annotates 6 orphan source_databases rows by prepending an explanatory note to the license field (keeping the rows, not deleting them). In --inventory-only mode writes a JSON report; in --apply mode updates the rows inside one transaction and writes a JSON report, requiring a pg_dump backup file path.
Command-line tool for managing a knowledge base, including indexing, searching, collections, health checks, server startup, and evaluation tasks.
A command-line tool to query and manage the German Regulatory Discovery Registry, including seeding data, searching industries, viewing obligations, and exporting the registry.
Seeds the industry taxonomy by populating the registry with top-level sections and divisions from the official German WZ 2008 classification system.
Seeds universal regulatory obligations that apply to all German businesses, forming the baseline legal requirements before adding industry-specific rules.
Strips brand identity from .pptx/.ppsx files by removing images, blanking text and metadata, and dropping custom parts, then outputs clean .potx template files.

Checks a .potx or .pptx file for residual brand leaks by scanning text/attributes for brand tokens, counting forbidden structural shapes, detecting banned parts, and verifying python-pptx can parse it.
Build a single versatile slide master by slicing 20 chosen layouts out of a sanitized PowerPoint template, renumbering them, and cleaning up all related metadata files.

Scans all .pptx files in a given directory and reports a table of each file's layout inventory (file name, slide size, number of masters, number of layouts, and counts of layout types), plus a coverage score showing how many of 14 canonical layout purposes each file's layouts satisfy.
Generates a beautiful 'Soll ich KI einsetzen?' decision cheatsheet as an SVG file, with optional PDF conversion.
Generates an A0-sized SVG poster showing 47 technology evolution paths converging into the smartphone, with optional PDF conversion.
Generates a marketing strategy and launch roadmap poster (page 2) that complements a Five Forces and SWOT analysis poster (page 1), outputting a PDF.
Generates a DIN A1 poster analyzing the Math Art Etsy market using Five Forces and SWOT frameworks, outputting a PDF.
Downloads files from a list of URLs into a specified folder, skipping any that already exist.
Converts a PDF file to a Markdown file using the Gemini API, with retry logic and skipping already-converted files.
Parses a local BACKLOG.md file and sends all listed projects to a BB10 dashboard at localhost:5050.
Serves a dashboard on a local network for monitoring token usage and session data, accessible via a BlackBerry Leap browser.
Pulls live token/runway data from a local systray engine and pushes it to a BB10 dashboard, replacing a manual AppleScript.
An interactive command-line tool that helps you hand-label regex matches as true-positive or false-positive, then calculates precision and writes summary results.

Ingest Claude Code conversation transcripts into prompt_usage tables by walking project directories, classifying user messages against YAML patterns, and persisting matches with resumable and idempotent behavior.
Read session JSONL files from a directory, aggregate per-message token counts into hourly buckets, and write synthetic utilization snapshots into a token budget SQLite database.

Starts the Token Budget Engine server, which polls Claude usage data and serves it via HTTP, using an OAuth token for authentication. Accepts optional port, token, token-file, and database path arguments.
Scans workspace session JSONL files to extract file paths and determine the dominant project for each session, then writes the results to a session-projects.jsonl file.
Validates that SKILL.md and README.md documentation matches the actual CLI source code by checking flag declarations, command paths, positional arguments, and shell variable quoting.

Reads stdin and replaces Windows absolute paths (with single or double backslashes) with portable tokens, normalizes backslashes to forward slashes, and strips .exe suffixes, so goldens match across Windows and Linux.

Validates that SKILL.md and README.md documentation matches the actual CLI source code by running five checks: flag names, flag commands, positional arguments, shell variable quoting, and unknown commands.
Scans files changed between two git refs for supply-chain security issues, returning findings with exit code 0 if none are block-severity or 1 if at least one is.
Syncs app node data from a port manager database into a TypeScript manifest, caching icons and health endpoints via HTTP probing.
Generates a styled PDF document for consulting pricing, specifically for a Claude Code Kickstart SMB Package, with a formatted table.
Convert an Excel file's Learning Path sheet into a JSON curriculum file, optionally accepting a custom input path.
Starts the Codex usage engine server on a configurable port (default 17421) with rotating log files stored in ~/Library/Logs/CodexUsageSystray.
Polls Codex plugin job state files and sends ntfy notifications when background tasks (rescue/review) reach a terminal state.

Parses Claude platform findings, sanitizes them, generates embeddings, retrieves same-topic CLAUDE.md rules, classifies contradictions, stores results in Qdrant, writes a digest report, and sends a notification via ntfy.
Reads markdown memory files and insights JSONL, generates embeddings via local Ollama, and upserts them to a remote Qdrant vector database on a VPS via SSH tunnel.
An interactive terminal tool that samples regex matches and unmatched messages, lets you label each as true/false positive, then computes per-pattern precision and writes a pass/fail verdict.

Ingest Claude Code conversation transcripts into prompt_usage tables by walking project directories, classifying user messages against YAML patterns, and persisting matches with resumable watermark support.
Starts a background HTTP server and polling thread for tracking Claude usage, requiring an OAuth token and optional database path.
Run database schema migrations on a portmgr.db file.
Compares port declarations from watchdog DAGs against a portmgr database to detect and optionally auto-correct drift, and can also check live TCP sockets for squatters or unregistered ports.

Register an externally-generated poster HTML file into poster-engine history by saving a record to the database, and optionally copy the file to the output directory for web UI serving. Returns the final path of the poster file.
A CLI tool for summarizing persona manifests and validating artifact YAML definitions in the Persona Expert System, supporting machine-readable JSON output.
Poster Engine is a configurable infographic generator with commands for creating users, exporting posters, running batch generation, listing templates, reformatting posters, and managing users.
Runs the poster_engine CLI main function, which processes input arguments to generate poster output files.
Runs a single worker that consumes and processes PosterEngine generation jobs from a queue, typically deployed as a separate container or process.
Seeds initial data for the Persona Expert System, running idempotently to add required setup data like industry tags or system personas.
Reads a topic calendar YAML file, checks the daily generation budget, and writes a sorted topics.csv for the next pipeline step.
Evaluates poster quality from PosterEngine by batch ID or poster IDs, outputting pass/fail results as JSON.
Formats pipeline results as a markdown summary and posts it as a new issue on Paperclip.
Publishes passing posters and exports them as PNG images, returning a summary.
unknown purpose (consigliere-pp-cli)
unknown purpose (hermes-pp-cli)
unknown purpose (poster-engine-pp-cli)
Pull items from Consigliere API for a specified week (defaults to current week).
Builds an index of methodology cards by parsing front matter from markdown files.
Append a procedural gap record in JSONL format to a log file, capturing a stage, symptom, root cause, workaround, proposed fix, severity, and free-form evidence.
Appends a single row of data from a JSON string to a CSV file (defaults to results.csv).
Sync airline audit results to an Obsidian vault by writing markdown notes for assessments, session handovers, or learnings.
Manage airline audit status in a YAML file by marking airlines as done, failed, or in progress, and tracking batch progress with a counter.
Prints a summary of audit progress from a YAML file, defaulting to airlines.yaml.
Generates a self-contained HTML poster from a BPMN process JSON file, embedding Camunda BPMN icons as SVG symbols.
Converts an acme-cert-poster.html file into a BPMN 2.0 XML file that can be imported into Visio, Camunda Modeler, or bpmn.io.
Converts an ACME certificate poster HTML file into a draw.io diagram file (.drawio) by extracting process data via a Node.js mock DOM and building mxGraph XML with native draw.io shapes.
Exports an ACME certificate poster HTML file to two Mermaid diagram files: a high-level overview and a detailed process flow with subgraphs.
Export an HTML poster to a Visio .vsdx file by extracting process data and element positions via Node, then building the Visio file with XML injection.
Executes Manim animation code and cleans up the temporary directory used for rendering.
A CLI tool for managing sidequest skills, including listing, detailing, scoring, rolling back, and running an autopilot engine.
Rebuilds acme-cert-process.json from the authoritative poster HTML by running a Node script to extract data, then writing the JSON output.
Generates a DIN A1 landscape poster PDF that maps the architecture, regulatory constraints, modules, and autoresearch evolution of a Steuerberater Document Processing Solution.
Launches a local web dashboard for browsing and managing STEUB use cases, served via FastAPI and accessible at http://localhost:8501.
A command-line tool for managing a FastAPI server and performing database operations like restore, repair, and reset.
Command-line tool for managing a skill catalog, including taxonomy management, data loading, health checks, and quality scoring.
A command-line tool for managing skill provenance, including license scanning, similarity checking, ingestion, auditing, benchmarking, and gap analysis.
Generates a DIN A1 landscape poster PDF that maps the architecture, regulatory constraints, modules, and autoresearch evolution of a Steuerberater Document Processing Solution.
Installs Empirica with Claude Code plugin, empirica-framework skill, environment variable configuration, and optional Qdrant + Ollama embeddings setup on Linux, macOS, or Windows.
A lightweight HTTPS proxy server that handles Etsy OAuth2 callbacks on a VPS and relays tokens to a local development machine, supporting both mock and live modes.

Manage Etsy OAuth tokens locally by communicating with a relay server on a VPS, supporting commands to check status, start authentication, refresh tokens, print the access token, poll for token availability, and test the token against the Etsy API.
Scrapes commercial-use dingbat fonts from dafont.com, downloads ZIPs, extracts font files, and imports them into the SVG-PAINT font pipeline.
Scrapes commercial-use dingbat fonts from 1001fonts.com, downloads ZIPs, extracts font files, and imports them into the SVG-PAINT font pipeline.
Scrapes commercial-use dingbat fonts from FontSpace, downloads and extracts them, then imports verified fonts into SVG-PAINT via API.
Reads Markdown segments from docs/copy/, assembles them into a Jinja2 HTML template, and renders the result to a PDF file using WeasyPrint.

Analyzes Python source files to build a JSON connection graph showing which functions are already resolved (skip) vs unresolved (analyze), based on direct calls, decorator registries, importlib dispatch, and event bus subscriptions.

Scans the codebase for API endpoints and JavaScript fetch calls, then writes two new sheets ('Wiring Status' and 'Code Scan') into an Excel audit file to show which endpoints are wired, partially wired, or have gaps.
Generates SVG files of geometric solids using a procedural isometric engine, validates them, and optionally stores them as database records.

Parses a specification markdown file, extracts target files, checks that referenced functions exist in those files via AST, and runs micro-prompts against an API router to detect KNOWN_PATTERNS or ARCHITECTURE violations, outputting JSON findings.
Runs rembg (u2net model) on 5 test images, outputs RGBA PNGs to a specified directory, and records timing per image.
Migrates data from a v1 Windows SQLite database to a v2 Mac database, converting fonts, glyphs, collections, and their relationships while skipping empty or irrelevant records.

Reads VisualBriefs from data/p0_briefs/, converts their suggested_sub_clouds into CloudTemplate JSON (renaming control_points to points), and saves the templates to data/p0_templates/ for use with compose_clouds().
Populates license_type, source_url, and eua_text fields for all FontSources based on their provider field, using a hardcoded mapping of provider names to license metadata.

Runs an extended parameter sweep for the vtracer image tracer, testing additional filter_speckle, corner_threshold, and length_threshold values beyond the original 135-run matrix, and saves the resulting SVG files and a CSV matrix.
Reads key files from a project, extracts function signatures and detect/action function bodies, and injects them into a prompt template for use with Ollama.
Generates a blind A/B comparison HTML report that pairs composed SVG command sequences with hand-coded generators, randomizing labels so reviewers cannot distinguish which is which.
Normalizes traced SVG files to a 100x100 viewBox by selecting the best SVG per image (lowest path count from a CSV matrix), applying scale transforms, and recording quality metrics.
Checks whether a SQLite database is safe to migrate by verifying no concurrent writer exists and the database passes a quick integrity check, returning a specific exit code for each condition.
Runs a parameter sweep of the vtracer tool on background-removed images, tracing each combination of settings to SVG and recording path count and file size in a CSV.
Sends a prompt to LM Studio's local OpenAI-compatible chat endpoint and returns the response text along with elapsed time.
Downloads reference images from Wikimedia Commons for all subjects in a taxonomy, organizing them into code-based folders and recording metadata.
Analyzes Python source files to build a JSON connection graph of function reachability, helping an LLM determine which functions are already resolved versus unresolved.
Launches a floating toolbar GUI for SVG-PAINT on macOS, providing a tkinter-based interface equivalent to the Windows PowerShell toolbar.
Batch renders 200 SVG images (20 subjects × 10 seeds each) from CloudTemplate files, saving them as numbered seed variants per subject folder.
Downloads 5 test images (elephant, apple, car, star, tree) from Wikimedia Commons for a raster-to-SVG tracing comparison spike, saving them as JPG files to a local directory.
Downloads reference images from Wikimedia Commons for 20 bootstrap subjects, saving them to local folders and generating a metadata CSV with source URLs and licenses.
Generates structured visual briefs (JSON) for bootstrap subjects by analyzing reference images using a local vision model via LM Studio.
Generates a branded PDF from Markdown content, applying hardcoded brand tokens and supporting special inline extensions for badges, callouts, formulas, and sources.
Downloads reference images that are currently stored as URLs to local storage and updates their file paths.
Serves a dashboard HTML page and a JSON endpoint showing production pipeline validation status for mobile.
Generate structured JSON documents (VisualBriefs) describing visual characteristics, proportions, and control points for 20 bootstrap subjects, with an optional dry-run flag to print without writing.
Manage the TODO-Today/DONE-Today lifecycle: move completed tasks, archive done items, sync to backlog, and generate daily summaries.
Generate a promo PPTX file for a given collection ID (synchronous, blocking operation).
Run integrity checks on the live SQLite database and optionally on corrupted backup files, and list corrupted database files with nearby git commits based on file modification time.
Generate PDFs for collections using templates, including individual collections, all collections, contact sheets, Etsy listing PDFs, and color flyers.
Analyze and reverse-engineer style from SVG files and/or atom IDs, with optional JSON output.
Adapts safari configuration files to multiple age tiers, optionally in batches, and outputs the adapted configs to a specified directory.
Manage theme packs by cloning, importing, regenerating, or previewing collections.
Generates and uploads all listing images for a given Etsy collection. Takes a collection ID and optional shop config ID, outputs a text confirmation and writes image files to Etsy.
A command-line tool that provides pull, serve, and status commands for a self-healing CI pipeline, automatically testing, fixing, and restarting a server when failures are detected.
Manage PDF atoms in a collection: list atoms, upload a local PDF file, delete an atom and its source file, or export the collection as a ZIP file.
Pre-generate branded care card PDFs with a light background, optionally filtering by language (DE, EN, or all).
Manage translation IDs and workflow across language directories: auto-discover languages, validate ID parity, remove [DRAFT] headers, and show translation status per block.
Extract a style recipe from existing SVG files or SvgAtom IDs using an AI-powered Style Analyzer, and save the result to a JSON file.
Generate a color palette from a named preset (e.g., warm_earth, ocean) with options for background, color count, audience tier, and naming, and optionally save it as ColorScheme objects.
Generate a branded A4 product overview PDF for a collection, with options for language, output path, and opening the file after creation.
Adapt a recipe configuration file from one developmental age tier to another, using a specified plugin.
Trigger a spider discovery sweep, optionally for a single subject or as a dry run.
Creates a bundle ZIP from multiple collections, or validates collections and shows glyph counts in dry-run mode.
Runs structural validation on Safari configuration files, outputting a JSON report to a specified path and optionally failing on violations.
A command-line tool for managing SVG assets, including importing fonts, syncing icon sources, running taxonomy operations, and performing A/B testing for Safari elephant designs.
Fetches images, traces them with vtracer, scores quality, and mutates parameters via hill-climbing over multiple iterations to find the best parameter set, optionally saving it as a Generator Recipe.
Run a taxonomy enrichment sweep with options for mode, target codes, proposal limit, and dry-run preview.
Generate Etsy product bundle directories from adapted safari cards, with configurable output and bundle strategy.

A Typer-based CLI that inspects, configures, and operates on entities (collections, sets, schemes, atoms, tags, plugins, fonts, production runs) via sub-apps. It accepts paths, strings, ints, and JSON arguments and outputs paths, JSON, or text.
Generates an SVG from a natural language description using an LLM and command executor pipeline.
Fetches analytics data from Google Analytics 4 using the Data API, with authentication via service account credentials from environment variables.
Analyzes Google Analytics data to compare periods, detect trends, generate performance insights, and provide automated recommendations.
A command-line tool for generating, validating, and exporting UTM tracking parameters and campaign URLs for marketing campaigns.
Creates a new skill directory from a template, converting the skill name to Title Case and generating a SKILL.md file.
Packages a skill folder into a distributable .skill file, optionally specifying an output directory.
Quickly validates a skill by checking that its SKILL.md file exists.
Creates or removes a mock project with a git repository, sample source files, and handoff documents for testing the session-handoff skill.
Creates a new handoff document with auto-detected metadata like timestamp, project path, git branch, recent git log, modified files, and handoff chain linking.
Lists handoff documents in a project's .claude/handoffs/ directory, showing each file's date, title, and completion status.
Checks how outdated a handoff document is by analyzing time since creation, recent git commits, changed files, and branch divergence.
Validate a handoff document for completeness and quality by checking for TODO placeholders, required sections, potential secrets, referenced file existence, and calculating a quality score.
Provides helper functions for common Shopify GraphQL operations, including query templates, pagination helpers, and rate limit handling.
Interactive script to scaffold Shopify apps, extensions, or themes, with environment variable loading from multiple locations.
Generate or edit images using the OpenAI Image API, defaulting to gpt-image-1.5 with structured prompt augmentation.
